metadata.title: Inverse Approximation Theory for Nonlinear Recurrent Neural Networks
organizations: 1Department of Mathematics, National University of Singapore

sections.title: Abstract
content: We prove an inverse approximation theorem for the approximation of nonlinear sequence-to-sequence relationships using recurrent neural networks (RNNs). This is a so-called Bernstein-type result in approximation theory, which deduces properties of a target function under the assumption that it can be effectively approximated by a hypothesis space. In particular, we show that nonlinear sequence relationships that can be stably approximated by nonlinear RNNs must have an exponential decaying memory structure - a notion that can be made precise.

sections.title: Recurrent neural networks
content:
Let m be the hidden dimension. With trainable weights c, W, U and activation σ,

                    yk= cThk(2)

sections.title: Stable approximation
content: Definition 1 (Stable approximation via parameterized models). Let β0> 0. We say a target functional sequence H admits a β0-stable approximation under {H(m)}, if there exists a sequence of parameterized approximants Hm= H(·, θm), θm∈ Θmwhich are continuous in input x in point-wise topology.
The following two graphs are perturbation errors for exponential decaying memory and polynomial decaying memory.

figures.description: Perturbation error for exponential decaying memory showing stable region below beta_0
Perturbation error for polynomial decaying memory showing instability as beta increases

sections.title: Memory function
content: Definition 2 (Memory function of nonlinear functional sequences). For continuous, causal, regular and time-homogeneous functional sequences H, define the memory function of H over bounded Heaviside input uτ.

This memory function definition is a natural extension for the function ρ in linear functional. For continuous, causal, regular and time-homogeneous functional sequences H, we say it has a decaying memory if the limit of the memory function goes to zero as t approaches infinity.

sections.title: Main theorems
content: Theorem 3 (Approximation Theorem - Curse of memory in nonlinear RNNs). Assume H is a sequence of continuous, causal, regular and time-homogeneous functionals on X with decaying memory. Suppose there exists a sequence of hardtanh RNNs stably approximating H. Then, the memory function of the nonlinear functional decays exponentially.

sections.title: Curse of memory phenomenon in nonlinear RNNs
content: For nonlinear functionals with polynomial decaying memory function and tanh RNNs:

figures.description: Graph showing perturbation error E(beta) vs perturbation beta for different hidden dimensions m, illustrating the curse of memory

sections.title: Reparameterization enables stable approximation
content: For linear RNNs, stable approximation of target functionals with polynomial decaying memory can be achieved via reparameterization of the recurrent weight matrix W. Exponential parameterization W = -eMand softplus parameterization W = -log(eM+ I) are shown:

figures.description: Perturbation error plot for exponential reparameterization
Perturbation error plot for softplus reparameterization

sections.title: Conclusion
content: We derive the first known Bernstein-type result in the setting of sequence modelling using nonlinear RNNs. We show that, assuming a given target sequence relationship can be stably approximated by RNNs with hardtanh/tanh-like activations, then the target functional sequence's memory structure must be exponentially decreasing.
